Rules.title: How roller derby works
Rules.lead: Never watched a derby bout before? A couple of minutes and you'll know exactly what you're looking at.
Rules.basicsBody: Two teams of five skaters race counter-clockwise around a flat, oval track, with plenty of physical contact. A game is made up of short sprints of up to 2 minutes: the jam. Both teams put one point-scorer and four defenders on the track at the same time. The defenders skate together as one group, the pack, which the other team's jammer has to break through.
Rules.rolesBody: You can tell everyone apart by their helmet. Watch for the star and the stripe.
Rules.jammerDesc: Star on the helmet. The only player who scores points.
Rules.pivotDesc: Stripe on the helmet. Leads the defence and can take over the jammer role via a star pass.
Rules.blockerDesc: Stops the opposing jammer and helps their own jammer through. Together the blockers form the pack.
Rules.scoringBody: On the first pass the jammer breaks through the pack, with no points yet. After that every opponent passed scores 1 point. Whoever breaks through the pack legally first becomes lead jammer and can call off the jam early to deny the other team points.
Rules.penaltiesBody: Illegal hits, back blocks, trips or playing out of bounds earn a penalty: 30 seconds in the penalty box. A team skating short-handed makes it much easier for the other team to score.
Rules.formatBody: The Small Teams Tournament runs short 20-minute bouts (no halftime): a compact take on the regular 60-minute game. Otherwise the official rules apply.
Rules.sourcesBody: This tournament follows the official ruleset of the WFTDA (Women's Flat Track Derby Association), which also has in-depth explanations and visuals about the sport.

Venue.title: Venue & info
Venue.houseIntro: So everyone has a good day, we keep to a few agreements inside the hall.
Venue.houseDefault1: Respect skaters, officials and the audience.
Venue.houseDefault2: No food or drinks in the hall except water in a closable bottle.
Venue.houseDefault3: Keep bags out of the track-side area, use the locker rooms.
Venue.houseDefault5: First aid or questions? Head to the organizer's table by the entrance.
Venue.foodBody: The sports hall has a canteen with coffee, soft drinks, snacks and treats. Inside the playing area only water (closable bottle).
Venue.firstAidBody: The first aid post is marked with a green cross near the entrance. In an emergency head to the organizer's table.
Venue.changingBody: There are gendered and gender-neutral locker rooms. Valuables? Lock them up — organizers aren't responsible.
Venue.parkingBody: Free parking around the venue. From Nijmegen Central Station take bus 6 towards Weezenhof, stop Horstacker.
Venue.sponsorsBody: This tournament is supported by our sponsors. Logos follow closer to the event date.

Notifications.title: Notifications
Notifications.subtitle: Turn on notifications so you don't miss anything on the day: "bout starting soon", results, photo contest winner and more.
Notifications.tip: Tip: add this app to your home screen for the best experience. On iPhone: Safari → Share → "Add to Home Screen". On Android: Chrome menu → "Install app".
Notifications.unsupported: Your browser doesn't support push notifications. Use Chrome, Firefox or Safari 16+.
Notifications.denied: Notifications are blocked. Enable them in your browser settings and reload this page.

Toernooi.title: About the tournament
Toernooi.lead: Everything you need to know about the Small Teams Tournament by Roadkill Rollers Nijmegen.
Toernooi.introHeading: What is the Small Teams Tournament?
Toernooi.introBody: The Small Teams Tournament is a roller derby tournament for small teams. In short, fast matches the teams face off against each other. A fun day full of derby, for players and spectators alike.
Toernooi.formatBody: Throughout the day every team plays several short matches. New to roller derby? On the rules page we explain what to look for in just a few minutes.
Toernooi.dayBody: Arrive on time, bring your water bottle and a good mood. There is plenty to enjoy around the track: cheer on the teams, vote for the MVP and join in on the derby bingo. Practical info about the venue is on the location page.
